annotations.label: Permutation (two meanings)

anchor.snippet: a permutation of a set can mean one of two different things

annotations.note: Mathlib formalizes the bijection meaning as `Equiv.Perm`, but the informal second meaning (ordered arrangement) has no single named counterpart.

annotations.label: Six permutations of {1,2,3}

anchor.snippet: An example of the first meaning is the six permutations

annotations.note: This is an illustrative worked example, not a declaration; the general count is `Fintype.card_perm`.

annotations.label: Number of permutations is n!

anchor.snippet: The number of permutations of n distinct objects is n factorial

annotations.note: `Fintype.card_perm` states `Fintype.card (Perm α) = (Fintype.card α)!`.

annotations.label: Permutation as bijection

anchor.snippet: a permutation of a set S is defined as a bijection from S to itself

annotations.note: `Equiv.Perm α` is defined as the type of bijections `α ≃ α`.

anchors.snippet: a permutation of a set S is defined as a bijection from S to itself

annotations.label: Permutation (3,1,2) as function

anchor.snippet: the permutation (3, 1, 2) corresponds to the function

annotations.note: A concrete illustrative example, not a Mathlib declaration.

anchors.snippet: the permutation (3, 1, 2) corresponds to the function

annotations.label: Symmetric group

anchor.snippet: The collection of all permutations of a set form a group called the symmetric group of the set

annotations.note: `Equiv.permGroup` gives the group structure on `Equiv.Perm α`, i.e. the symmetric group.

anchors.snippet: The collection of all permutations of a set form a group called the symmetric group of the set

annotations.label: k-permutations

anchor.snippet: the k -permutations , or partial permutations , are the ordered arrangements of k distinct elements selected from a set

annotations.note: k-permutations correspond to embeddings `Fin k ↪ S`, counted by `Fintype.card_embedding_eq`, but there is no dedicated named definition.

annotations.label: Permutation as bijection

anchor.snippet: A permutation can be defined as a bijection

annotations.note: `Equiv.Perm α` is exactly a bijection from a set to itself.

anchors.snippet: A permutation can be defined as a bijection

annotations.label: Identity permutation

anchor.snippet: The identity permutation is defined by

annotations.note: `Equiv.Perm.one_def` identifies the group identity `1` with `Equiv.refl α`, the identity permutation.

anchors.snippet: The identity permutation is defined by

annotations.label: Symmetric group structure

anchor.snippet: The set of all permutations of a set with n elements forms the symmetric group

annotations.note: `Equiv.permGroup` provides the symmetric-group structure on permutations of a finite set.

anchors.snippet: The set of all permutations of a set with n elements forms the symmetric group

annotations.label: Composition not commutative

anchor.snippet: composition of two permutations is not commutative

annotations.note: Mathlib has no lemma asserting that permutation composition fails to commute in general.

anchors.snippet: composition of two permutations is not commutative

annotations.label: Active permutation

anchor.snippet: termed an active permutation or substitution

annotations.note: This is informal terminology with no corresponding Mathlib declaration.

annotations.label: Passive permutation

anchor.snippet: called a passive permutation

annotations.note: Informal terminology with no corresponding Mathlib declaration.

annotations.label: Decomposition into disjoint cycles

anchor.snippet: can be decomposed into one or more disjoint cycles

annotations.note: `cycleFactorsFinset_noncommProd` shows a permutation equals the product of its disjoint cycle factors.

annotations.label: k-cycle

anchor.snippet: A cycle consisting of k elements is called a k -cycle

annotations.note: `Equiv.Perm.IsCycle` captures cycles; the parameter k is recorded as the support cardinality / cycleType entry.

annotations.label: Fixed point

anchor.snippet: A fixed point of a permutation

annotations.note: `Function.IsFixedPt f x` is defined as `f x = x`, a fixed point.

annotations.label: Derangement

anchor.snippet: A permutation with no fixed points is called a derangement

annotations.note: `derangements α` is the set of permutations with no fixed points.

annotations.label: Transposition

anchor.snippet: is called a transposition

annotations.note: `Equiv.swap a b` is the transposition exchanging two elements (also `Equiv.Perm.IsSwap`).

annotations.label: Two-line notation

anchor.snippet: lists the elements of S in the first row

annotations.note: A notational convention with no Mathlib formalization.

annotations.label: Two-line notation example

anchor.snippet: the permutation of S = {1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6} given by the function

annotations.note: An illustrative example of notation, not a declaration.

annotations.label: One-line notation

anchor.snippet: write the permutation in one-line notation as

annotations.note: A notational convention with no Mathlib formalization.

annotations.label: Cycle notation

anchor.snippet: with an orbit being called a cycle

annotations.note: The orbit/cycle structure is formalized (`cycleOf`, `SameCycle`, `Cycle`), but cycle notation itself is not.

annotations.label: Disjoint cycles commute

anchor.snippet: While permutations in general do not commute, disjoint cycles do

annotations.note: `Equiv.Perm.Disjoint.commute` proves disjoint permutations commute.

anchors.snippet: While permutations in general do not commute, disjoint cycles do

annotations.label: Inverse via reversing cycles

anchor.snippet: inverting the permutation is given by reversing the order of the elements in each cycle

annotations.note: `formPerm_reverse` shows reversing a cycle yields the inverse permutation.

anchors.snippet: inverting the permutation is given by reversing the order of the elements in each cycle

annotations.label: Canonical cycle notation

anchor.snippet: The canonical cycle notation is defined by

annotations.note: A notational convention with no Mathlib formalization.

annotations.label: Composition (rightmost first)

anchor.snippet: The rightmost permutation is applied to the argument first

annotations.note: `Equiv.Perm.mul_apply` gives `(f * g) x = f (g x)`, i.e. rightmost-first composition.

annotations.label: Composition (leftmost first)

anchor.snippet: the leftmost permutation acts first

annotations.note: `Equiv.trans` composes leftmost-first: `(e₁.trans e₂) x = e₂ (e₁ x)`.

annotations.label: Composition satisfies group axioms

anchor.snippet: The function composition operation satisfies the axioms of a group

annotations.note: `Equiv.permGroup` is the `Group` instance for permutations under composition.

annotations.label: k-permutation of n

anchor.snippet: means an ordered arrangement (list) of a k -element subset of an n -set

annotations.note: k-permutations of n correspond to embeddings `Fin k ↪ Fin n`; no dedicated named definition exists but their count is formalized.

anchors.snippet: means an ordered arrangement (list) of a k -element subset of an n -set

annotations.label: Number of k-permutations

anchor.snippet: The number of such k -permutations

annotations.note: `card_embedding_eq` gives `‖α ↪ β‖ = ‖β‖.descFactorial ‖α‖`, the falling-factorial count of k-permutations.

anchors.snippet: The number of such k -permutations

annotations.label: Pochhammer / falling factorial

anchor.snippet: it is known as the Pochhammer symbol

annotations.note: `descPochhammer` is the falling-factorial polynomial (with `Nat.descFactorial` as the numeric version).

anchors.snippet: it is known as the Pochhammer symbol

annotations.label: k-combination

anchor.snippet: a k-combination of a set S is a k- element (unordered) subset of S

annotations.note: `Finset.powersetCard k s` is the set of k-element subsets of s.

anchors.snippet: a k-combination of a set S is a k- element (unordered) subset of S

annotations.label: Relation of combinations to permutations

anchor.snippet: The number of k -combinations of an n -set

annotations.note: `card_powersetCard` gives `#(powersetCard n s) = s.card.choose n`.

anchors.snippet: The number of k -combinations of an n -set

annotations.label: Binomial coefficients

anchor.snippet: These numbers are also known as binomial coefficients

annotations.note: `Nat.choose n k` is the binomial coefficient.

anchors.snippet: These numbers are also known as binomial coefficients

annotations.label: k-tuples

anchor.snippet: are called k -tuples

annotations.note: k-tuples over S are simply elements of `Fin k → S` (or `S^k`), a basic type that is not separately named.

annotations.label: Number of k-tuples

anchor.snippet: the number of k -tuples over S is

annotations.note: `card_pi_const` gives `card (Fin n → α) = card α ^ n`, the count of k-tuples.

annotations.label: Multiset permutation

anchor.snippet: a multiset permutation is an ordered arrangement of elements of M

annotations.note: Mathlib has `List.permutations` for orderings of lists but no dedicated multiset-permutation definition.

anchors.snippet: a multiset permutation is an ordered arrangement of elements of M

annotations.label: Multinomial count of multiset permutations

anchor.snippet: the number of multiset permutations of M is given by a multinomial coefficient

annotations.note: `Multiset.countPerms` is defined as the multinomial coefficient, but its equality to the cardinality of distinct arrangements is not established.

anchors.snippet: the number of multiset permutations of M is given by a multinomial coefficient

annotations.label: Anagrams of MISSISSIPPI

anchor.snippet: the number of distinct anagrams of the word MISSISSIPPI is

annotations.note: A concrete numerical example, not a Mathlib declaration.

anchors.snippet: the number of distinct anagrams of the word MISSISSIPPI is

annotations.label: k-permutation of a multiset

anchor.snippet: A k -permutation of a multiset M is a sequence of k elements of M

annotations.note: No Mathlib definition for k-permutations of a multiset.

annotations.label: Generating function count

anchor.snippet: the number of permutations can be determined with generating functions

annotations.note: The generating-function approach to counting multiset permutations is not formalized.

annotations.label: MISSISSIPPI generating function

anchor.snippet: in the case of the multiset of letters in MISSISSIPPI

annotations.note: A worked generating-function example, not a declaration.

anchors.snippet: in the case of the multiset of letters in MISSISSIPPI

annotations.label: Circular permutation

anchor.snippet: An arrangement of distinct objects in a circular manner is called a circular permutation

annotations.note: Mathlib has no definition of circular permutations.

annotations.label: Equivalence of circular permutations

anchor.snippet: Two circular permutations are equivalent if one can be rotated into the other

annotations.note: No formalization of rotational equivalence of circular permutations.

annotations.label: Count of circular permutations

anchor.snippet: circular permutations of a set with n elements

annotations.note: The (n-1)! count of circular permutations is not formalized.

annotations.label: Number of permutations is n!

anchor.snippet: The number of permutations of n distinct objects is n !

annotations.note: `Fintype.card_perm` states `Fintype.card (Perm α) = (Fintype.card α)!`.

annotations.label: Stirling number of the first kind

anchor.snippet: The number of n -permutations with k disjoint cycles is the signless Stirling number of the first kind

annotations.note: `Nat.stirlingFirst` defines the unsigned Stirling numbers by recurrence but is not proven to count permutations with k cycles.

annotations.label: Cycle type

anchor.snippet: which is called the cycle type

annotations.note: `Equiv.Perm.cycleType` is the multiset of cycle lengths of a permutation.

annotations.label: Count of permutations of given cycle type

anchor.snippet: The number of permutations of a given cycle type is

annotations.note: `card_of_cycleType` gives the exact count `n!/(...)` of permutations of a prescribed cycle type.

annotations.label: Number of cycle types is partition function

anchor.snippet: The number of cycle types of a set with n elements equals the value of the partition function

annotations.note: `Equiv.Perm.partition` maps a permutation to a `Nat.Partition`, but the explicit count of cycle types as p(n) is not stated.

annotations.label: Cycle index polynomial

anchor.snippet: Polya 's cycle index polynomial is a generating function which counts permutations by their cycle type

annotations.note: Pólya's cycle index polynomial is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Conjugate of a permutation

anchor.snippet: is the conjugate of

annotations.note: Conjugation τστ⁻¹ is the general group notion `IsConj`, applicable to permutations.

annotations.label: Conjugacy iff same cycle type

anchor.snippet: two permutations are conjugate exactly when they have the same cycle type

annotations.note: `isConj_iff_cycleType_eq` proves two permutations are conjugate iff they have equal cycle type.

annotations.label: Order of a permutation

anchor.snippet: The order of a permutation

annotations.note: The order of a permutation is the general group-element order `orderOf`.

annotations.label: Order is lcm of cycle lengths

anchor.snippet: It is the least common multiple of the lengths of its cycles

annotations.note: `lcm_cycleType` proves `σ.cycleType.lcm = orderOf σ`.

annotations.label: Order example

anchor.snippet: For example, the order of

annotations.note: A concrete numerical example, not a declaration.

annotations.label: Permutation as product of transpositions

anchor.snippet: Every permutation of a finite set can be expressed as the product of transpositions

annotations.note: `truncSwapFactors` produces a list of transpositions whose product is the given permutation.

annotations.label: Parity well-defined (even/odd)

anchor.snippet: all permutations can be classified as even or odd

annotations.note: `Equiv.Perm.sign` is a well-defined monoid hom to `ℤˣ`, encoding the even/odd parity.

annotations.label: Sign of a permutation

anchor.snippet: assign a sign , written

annotations.note: `Equiv.Perm.sign` assigns the sign ±1 to each permutation.

annotations.label: Sign equals determinant of matrix

anchor.snippet: The sign of a permutation is equal to the determinant of its permutation matrix

annotations.note: `det_permutation` proves `det (σ.permMatrix R) = Perm.sign σ`.

annotations.label: Permutation matrix

anchor.snippet: A permutation matrix is an n × n matrix

annotations.note: `Equiv.Perm.permMatrix` is the permutation matrix associated to a permutation.

anchors.snippet: A permutation matrix is an n × n matrix

annotations.label: Matrix construction respects composition

anchor.snippet: this construction is compatible with composition of permutations

annotations.note: `permMatrixHom` is a monoid hom and `permMatrix_mul` shows compatibility with composition.

anchors.snippet: this construction is compatible with composition of permutations

annotations.label: Product of permutation matrices

anchor.snippet: For example, the one-line permutations

annotations.note: A concrete worked example, not a declaration.

anchors.snippet: For example, the one-line permutations

annotations.label: Inverse matrix convention

anchor.snippet: to find the inverse convention

annotations.note: `transpose_permMatrix` gives `(σ.permMatrix)ᵀ = σ⁻¹.permMatrix`, the math behind the inverse convention, but the notational discussion is informal.

annotations.label: Ascent

anchor.snippet: An ascent of a permutation

annotations.note: Permutation ascents are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Ascents of 3452167

anchor.snippet: the permutation 3452167 has ascents

annotations.note: An illustrative example for an unformalized concept.

annotations.label: Descent

anchor.snippet: a descent is a position i < n with

annotations.note: Permutation descents are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Ascending run

anchor.snippet: An ascending run of a permutation is a nonempty increasing contiguous subsequence

annotations.note: Ascending runs of permutations are not formalized.

annotations.label: Increasing subsequence

anchor.snippet: an increasing subsequence of a permutation is not necessarily contiguous

annotations.note: Increasing subsequences of permutations are not formalized as a definition in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Runs of 2453167

anchor.snippet: the permutation 2453167 has the ascending runs

annotations.note: An illustrative example for an unformalized concept.

annotations.label: Descents and ascending runs

anchor.snippet: then it must be the union of k ascending runs

annotations.note: The relation between descents and ascending runs is not formalized.

annotations.label: Eulerian number

anchor.snippet: is (by definition) the Eulerian number

annotations.note: Eulerian numbers are not formalized (Mathlib's `Eulerian` refers to graph trails).

annotations.label: Exceedance

anchor.snippet: An exceedance of a permutation

annotations.note: Exceedances of permutations are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Weak exceedance

anchor.snippet: then j is called a weak exceedance

annotations.note: Weak exceedances are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Exceedances equal descents

anchor.snippet: The number of n -permutations with k exceedances coincides with the number of n -permutations with k descents

annotations.note: This equidistribution result is not formalized.

annotations.label: Record (left-to-right maximum)

anchor.snippet: A record or left-to-right maximum of a permutation

annotations.note: Records / left-to-right maxima of permutations are not formalized.

annotations.label: Foata's fundamental bijection

anchor.snippet: Foata 's fundamental bijection transforms a permutation

annotations.note: Foata's fundamental bijection is not formalized in Mathlib.

anchors.snippet: Foata 's fundamental bijection transforms a permutation

annotations.label: Records equal cycles

anchor.snippet: the number of n -permutations with exactly k records is equal to the number of n -permutations with exactly k cycles

annotations.note: This equidistribution of records and cycles is not formalized.

anchors.snippet: the number of n -permutations with exactly k records is equal to the number of n -permutations with exactly k cycles

annotations.label: Foata maps weak exceedances to ascents

anchor.snippet: Foata's mapping takes an n -permutation with k weak exceedances to an n -permutation with k − 1 ascents

annotations.note: This property of Foata's mapping is not formalized.

anchors.snippet: Foata's mapping takes an n -permutation with k weak exceedances to an n -permutation with k − 1 ascents

annotations.label: Inversion

anchor.snippet: An inversion of a permutation

annotations.note: Permutation inversions are not a named definition; `sign` is built on inversion-counting internally but the count itself is not exposed.

annotations.label: Inversions equal for σ and σ⁻¹

annotations.label: Sorting via adjacent transpositions

anchor.snippet: is always possible and requires a sequence of k such operations

annotations.note: The bubble-sort result linking inversion count to adjacent transpositions is not formalized.

annotations.label: Mahonian number

anchor.snippet: The number of permutations of n with k inversions is expressed by a Mahonian number

annotations.note: Mahonian numbers are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Kobayashi enumeration formula

anchor.snippet: Kobayashi (2011) proved the enumeration formula

annotations.note: Kobayashi's enumeration formula is not formalized.

anchor.snippet: given the sequence [1, 2, 3, 4]

annotations.label: Steinhaus–Johnson–Trotter property

anchor.snippet: any two consecutive permutations in its output differ by swapping two adjacent values
